question: What are the two main frequency ranges defined for 5G NR?
options: FR1 (700 MHz-3.5 GHz) and FR2 (3.5-6 GHz)

question: Which of the following are challenges associated with mmWave (FR2) frequencies? Choose all that apply.
options: Poor penetration through walls and obstacles
correct_answers: Poor penetration through walls and obstacles

question: Which type of MIMO uses a large number of antennas at the base station to simultaneously serve multiple users?
What is the main advantage of FR1 (sub-7 GHz) over FR2 (mmWave) for 5G deployments?
What is the main advantage of FR2 (mmWave) over FR1 for 5G deployments?
options: Very high bandwidth enabling peak data rates
correct_answers: Very high bandwidth enabling peak data rates

question: What technique is critical for mmWave 5G deployments to overcome high path loss?
Which MIMO configuration simultaneously serves multiple users on the same time-frequency resources?
How has the evolution of cellular antennas progressed toward 5G? Choose the best answer.
options: From single omnidirectional antennas to large multi-element active antenna arrays
From large arrays to small single antennas
From MIMO to SISO for better coverage
From active to passive antenna systems
From high-frequency to low-frequency operation only
correct_answers: From single omnidirectional antennas to large multi-element active antenna arrays

question: What is the mmWave frequency range defined for 5G FR2?
Which of the following statements about 5G NR frequency ranges are correct? Choose all that apply.
options: FR1 provides better coverage but lower peak data rates than FR2
FR2 provides higher bandwidth but shorter range than FR1
mmWave deployments typically require denser small cell infrastructure
FR1 is commonly used for wide-area macro cell coverage
FR2 frequencies can easily penetrate buildings without signal loss
correct_answers: FR1 provides better coverage but lower peak data rates than FR2
FR2 provides higher bandwidth but shorter range than FR1
mmWave deployments typically require denser small cell infrastructure
FR1 is commonly used for wide-area macro cell coverage
